title: Success Criteria, Baselines, and Prompt Evals · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Define and test a support-triage prompt before optimizing its wording. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a versioned prompt evaluation harness after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define the user, task, decision, acceptable output, and material failure modes before drafting a prompt.
Turn subjective quality goals into observable criteria and representative test cases.
Create a minimal baseline prompt before adding scaffolding or examples.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Task Contract evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Task Contract assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Define the user, task, decision, acceptable output, and material failure modes before drafting a prompt. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Compare plausible approaches against the decision, constraints, evidence quality, and cost of being wrong.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt success evals. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Task Contract, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a versioned prompt evaluation harness.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Prompt quality can be judged without a defined task outcome. This fails the Task Contract contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: OpenAI Codex Manual: Prompting and Best Practices (Verified current 2026-07-13) — Controls Codex prompting, Plan mode, AGENTS.md, verification, and surface guidance.

content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define the user, task, decision, acceptable output, and material failure modes before drafting a prompt.

stimulus.title: Judge Review case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt success evals. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: An LLM judge eliminates the need for calibrated human review.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine deterministic checks, rubric review, and human inspection without treating one judge as truth. Artifact under review: a versioned prompt evaluation harness.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Judge Review, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Judge Review applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Combine deterministic checks, rubric review, and human inspection without treating one judge as truth.
